company_context: Identity Security and Access Management Leader | CyberArk Skip to content Palo Alto Networks Completes Acquisition of CyberArk to Secure the AI Era. Read press release . ✕ CyberArk is now a Palo Alto Networks company. Together, we are building the next generation identity security platform for the AI era. Learn why we are better together CyberArk Announces Record Q4/FY25 Results Read Press Release 7X a Leader in the 2025 Gartner MQ for PAM Download Complimentary Copy IMPACT 2026 Registration Now Open! Sign Up Now for Best Rate Secure every identity — human, machine and AI — with the right level of privilege controls. Watch the Video Secure identities. Shut out attackers. Seamlessly secure identities throughout the cycle of accessing any resource across any infrastructure, including hybrid, SaaS and multi-cloud. The CyberArk identity security platform is the first line of defense against malicious actors and unauthorized access to protect what matters most. Request a Demo Seamless & secure access for all identities By combining secure SSO, Adaptive MFA, Lifecycle Management, Directory Services and User Behavior Analytics, we help you streamline operations and give users simple and secure access to resources—on-premises, cloud, hybrid—from any location, using any device. Intelligent privilege controls Apply world-class intelligent privilege controls across the IT estate, as well as differentiated controls to secure the unique needs of workforce users, third-party vendors, endpoints and machine identities as they access sensitive data. Flexible identity automation & orchestration Streamline HR processes, ensure users have the right access to the right resources, enable compliance with industry or government regulations, and improve efficiencies across the board with orchestration and lifecycle management, permissions and entitlements, and directory and federation services. Identity Security Defined Centered on intelligent privilege controls, identity security seamlessly secures access for all identities and flexibly automates the identity lifecycle with continuous threat detection and prevention — all with a unified approach. Explore the Platform Apply intelligent privilege controls across the entire identity lifecycle Expand intelligent privilege controls that were designed for the most privileged user, such as an admin, to a broader range of human and machine identities, whenever standing or just-in-time access is required. Workforce & Customer Access Ensure that the right users have secure access to the right resources at the right times, by protecting workforce and customer credentials and tightly controlling access to on-premises and cloud-based applications, services and IT infrastructure. Learn More Endpoint Privilege Security Take control over unmanaged privilege on the endpoints to significantly reduce the area of attack and defend from threats by removing local admin rights, enforcing role-specific least privilege and improving audit-readi

conversation.speaker: Seller
conversation.message: Hi, my name is Alex and I'm reaching out from DeepData AI. We're a data processing solution that helps companies like yours streamline their data operations and gain valuable insights. I came across CyberArk and was impressed with the work you're doing in identity security. I think our solution could be a great fit for your team.

conversation.speaker: Buyer
conversation.message: Ah, thanks for reaching out, Alex. I'm John, the CTO here at CyberArk. We're always looking for ways to improve our operations, but I have to say, our plate is pretty full right now with the recent acquisition by Palo Alto Networks. What makes you think DeepData AI is a good fit for us?

conversation.speaker: Seller
conversation.message: Well, John, from what I understand, CyberArk is looking to build the next generation identity security platform for the AI era. Our solution can help you process and analyze large amounts of data, which is critical for identity security. We've had success with similar companies in the past, and I think we could help you accelerate your goals.

conversation.speaker: Buyer
conversation.message: I see what you're saying, but we've already got a number of tools in place for data processing. We've invested heavily in our current infrastructure, and I'm not sure we're looking to add another vendor to the mix. Plus, I'm not sure what the budget looks like for new tools right now. We're still figuring out our priorities post-acquisition.

conversation.speaker: Seller
conversation.message: I understand your concerns, John. However, I'd like to propose that our solution can actually help you reduce costs and improve efficiency in the long run. We've had companies see significant ROI from implementing our solution. Would you be open to discussing this further and exploring how we can help?

conversation.speaker: Buyer
conversation.message: Honestly, Alex, I appreciate the pitch, but I don't have the budget authority to approve new tools. Our procurement process is pretty rigorous, and we'd need to go through a thorough evaluation before even considering a new vendor. And to be frank, we're not looking to add any new tools to our stack right now. We're focused on integrating our existing solutions and getting the most out of what we already have.

conversation.speaker: Seller
conversation.message: I understand, John. Can I at least send you some information about our solution and follow up with you in a few weeks to see if anything has changed?

conversation.speaker: Buyer
conversation.message: No, Alex, I think we're going to have to pass on DeepData AI for now. We appreciate the outreach, but we're just not in a position to consider new tools at this time. Thank you for your understanding.

analysis.raw:
Score: 2
Outcome: Failure
Key_Objection: Authority
Feedback: The seller should have asked about the procurement process and budget authority earlier in the conversation to avoid investing time in a pitch that was unlikely to succeed. Sentiment: Neutral
